2605.23175.title: Robust LLM Watermarking with Minimal Semantic Distortion for IP Protection
2605.23175.paper_summary: Proprietary large language models (LLMs) face risks of intellectual property (IP) violation, as adversaries can replicate an LLM by collecting input-output pairs to train a surrogate model, causing financial setbacks. Watermarks offer a promising defense to verify ownership, but existing methods often struggle with semantic distortion, factual inconsistency, and adversarial attacks. In addition, key-conditioned watermarks for provider-specific detection, especially in cross-provider and multi-user scenarios, remain largely underexplored. To address these challenges, we propose SAFESEAL, a novel key-conditioned watermarking framework that achieves strong detectability with minimal impact on model utility, effectively balancing detectability, utility, and robustness. SAFESEAL preserves named entities while substituting linguistic terms with context-aware synonyms through a key-conditioned Tournament sampling mechanism, maintaining semantic fidelity and factual consistency. For detection, we introduce a key-conditioned contrastive detector that jointly encodes the text and key, enabling provider-specific and robust watermark verification. We derive theoretical bounds on the utility-detectability trade-off and significantly reduce latency through lightweight models, batching, and parallelism. Extensive experiments show that SAFESEAL outperforms baselines in utility, detectability, and robustness, achieving a BERTScore of 0.983, entity similarity of 0.963, a 98.2% detection rate, and the highest human ratings for text quality and content preservation, with latency comparable to the fastest baseline. To promote transparency and community-driven progress, we release the first public watermark leaderboard and an interactive demo.
2605.23175.paper_summary_zh: 專有的大型語言模型（LLMs）面臨知識產權（IP）違規的風險，因為對手可以通過收集輸入-輸出對來訓練替代模型，從而造成財務損失。水印提供了一種有前景的防禦手段來驗證所有權，但現有方法常常在語義扭曲、事實不一致和對抗攻擊方面掙扎。此外，針對提供者特定檢測的關鍵條件水印，特別是在跨提供者和多用戶場景中，仍然在很大程度上未被探索。為了解決這些挑戰，我們提出了SAFESEAL，一種新穎的關鍵條件水印框架，能在對模型效用影響最小的情況下實現強檢測性，有效平衡檢測性、效用和穩健性。SAFESEAL在保持命名實體的同時，通過關鍵條件的錦標賽抽樣機制用上下文感知的同義詞替代語言術語，保持語義的忠實性和事實的一致性。為了檢測，我們引入了一種關鍵條件對比檢測器，該檢測器共同編碼文本和關鍵，實現提供者特定的穩健水印驗證。我們推導了效用-檢測性權衡的理論界限，並通過輕量級模型、批處理和並行處理顯著降低延遲。大量實驗表明，SAFESEAL在效用、檢測性和穩健性方面超越了基準，達到0.983的BERTScore、0.963的實體相似度、98.2%的檢測率，以及文本質量和內容保留的最高人類評分，延遲與最快的基準相當。為了促進透明度和社區驅動的進步，我們發布了首個公共水印排行榜和一個互動演示。
2605.23175.author: Kieu Dang et.al.
2605.23175.authors: Kieu Dang, Phung Lai, NhatHai Phan, Yelong Shen, Ruoming Jin
